January 3a, 1990
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Re: Recruirement to FiIe Form 721-
Ladies and Gentlemen:
We represent Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management.
Nicholas-Applegate is one of several investment managers retained
by the San Francisco City Employees, Retirement System. The
Retirement system has recentry reguested that the principals of
Nicholas-Applegate complete and file Form 72L, Statement of
Economic rnterests, on the basis that individuals who manag:e
investments for city and county agencies are reguired to comply
with Government Code Section 872OO as recently amended by
Assembly Bill No. 941.
Nicholas-Applegate is a private limited partnership,
with Nicholas-Applegate capital Management, rnc. acting as the
general partner. 
None of the linited partners or employees of
the general partner are officials, 
candidates or employees of any
state, county or city agency. we contacted the Fair political
Practices commission and spoke to Ms. Alice Hughes about why the
individuals who manage the Retirement System's investments are
reguired to make the same disclosures as public officials 
and
candj-dates. She indicated that Assembly BiIl No. g4L extended to
private individuals because of the inclusion of the word
"empJ-oyees.' We have reviewed Assembly BilI No. 94L and we can
find no basis for the commission's extension of the financial
disclosure reguirements to pri-vate individual-s.
Nicholas-Applegate, as an investment adviser registered
with the Securities and Exchange Comrnission under the Investment
Advisers Act of 1940 and with the securities authorities of
California and over forty other states, is already subject to
ffr
6
Lr\


GRAY, CARY, AMES 6 FRYE
Fair Political Practices Conmission
January 31, 1990
Page 2
significant regulatory oversight, including requj.red disclosure
to public agencies and clients. 
It would be very burdensome to
be required to make additional disclosures in every California
jurisdiction in which Nicholas-Applegate has public agency
clients, and such disclosure will be largely duplicative of what
is already publicly avail,able.
We respectfully request your advice and comment as to
the statutory and/or regulatory basis for the inclusion of
private individuals in the disclosure requirements, since the
only reference reguiring such disclcsure that we have lcca+-ed is
in the instructions to Form 721 which were published by your
agency.
We have not complied with the January 31, t99o filing
deadline due to the fact that the Form T2L package was delivered
to Nicholas-Applegate only within the last several days, however,
pending receipt of your response, we wiII promptly respond
accordingly.
I look forward to hearing from you.
Very truly yours,
,h".,f *4 h/l--
Margaret M.
For
GRAY, CARY,
Shea
AHES & T'RYE

sections.question_synthetic: Are private investment managers retained by public employee retirement systems required to file Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) under Government Code Section 8200 as amended by Assembly Bill 941?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question is embedded in the body of the letter. The letter references prior contact with FPPC staff (Ms. Alice Hughes) and requests advice regarding Form 721 filing requirements. Filing deadline was January 31, 1990, and compliance was deferred pending FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Are private investment managers retained by public employee retirement systems required to file Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) under Government Code Section 8200 as amended by Assembly Bill 941?

FRYE (r904- l97O) Gnnv, Cnnv, Aues & FnvE ATTORNEYS AT LAW ITOO FIRST INTERSTATE PLAZA 4OI B STREET SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92IOI.42I9 TELEPHoNE [ete] ooe-azoo rax [ere] z3s-4?37 rex [ors] a36- lo4a TELEX rr 9lO 335- 1273 OTHER OFFICES IN LA JOLLA EL CENTRO wRrrER's DtREcr LtNE (5Le) 6ee-3644 January 3a, 1990 Fair Political Practices Commission Technical Assistance and Analysis Division P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Re: Recruirement to FiIe Form 721- Ladies and Gentlemen: We represent Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management. Nicholas-Applegate is one of several investment managers retained by the San Francisco City Employees, Retirement System. The Retirement system has recentry reguested that the principals of Nicholas-Applegate complete and file Form 72L, Statement of Economic rnterests, on the basis that individuals who manag:e investments for city and county agencies are reguired to comply with Government Code Section 872OO as recently amended by Assembly Bill No. 941. Nicholas-Applegate is a private limited partnership, with Nicholas-Applegate capital Management, rnc. acting as the general partner. None of the linited partners or employees of the general partner are officials, candidates or employees of any state, county or city agency. we contacted the Fair political Practices commission and spoke to Ms. Alice Hughes about why the individuals who manage the Retirement System's investments are reguired to make the same disclosures as public officials and candj-dates. She indicated that Assembly BiIl No. g4L extended to private individuals because of the inclusion of the word "empJ-oyees.' We have reviewed Assembly BilI No. 94L and we can find no basis for the commission's extension of the financial disclosure reguirements to pri-vate individual-s. Nicholas-Applegate, as an investment adviser registered with the Securities and Exchange Comrnission under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 and with the securities authorities of California and over forty other states, is already subject to ffr 6 Lr\ GRAY, CARY, AMES 6 FRYE Fair Political Practices Conmission January 31, 1990 Page 2 significant regulatory oversight, including requj.red disclosure to public agencies and clients. It would be very burdensome to be required to make additional disclosures in every California jurisdiction in which Nicholas-Applegate has public agency clients, and such disclosure will be largely duplicative of what is already publicly avail,able. We respectfully request your advice and comment as to the statutory and/or regulatory basis for the inclusion of private individuals in the disclosure requirements, since the only reference reguiring such disclcsure that we have lcca+-ed is in the instructions to Form 721 which were published by your agency. We have not complied with the January 31, t99o filing deadline due to the fact that the Form T2L package was delivered to Nicholas-Applegate only within the last several days, however, pending receipt of your response, we wiII promptly respond accordingly. I look forward to hearing from you. Very truly yours, ,h".,f *4 h/l-- Margaret M. For GRAY, CARY, Shea AHES & T'RYE MMS: kII cc: Fred C. Applegate Arthur Nicholas Cameron Jay
embedding.summary: Law firm Gray, Cary, Ames & Frye requests FPPC guidance on whether Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management, a private investment adviser, must file Form 721 disclosures based on managing investments for the San Francisco City Employees' Retirement System. The firm argues that private individuals should not be subject to these requirements and seeks clarification of the statutory basis for including private managers in the disclosure requirements.
